title: Darcy-Weisbach Equations
text: Darcy-Weisbach equation universal theoretically rigorous formula calculating friction head loss pipe flow. Unlike empirical formulas like Hazen-Williams, Darcy-Weisbach applies fluid flow regime (laminar, transitional, turbulent). hf package, equation expressed using volumetric flow rate (QQ) consistency: hf=f8LQ2π2gD5h_f = f \frac{8 L Q^2}{\pi^2 g D^5} : hfh_f: Friction head loss (m) ff: Darcy friction factor (dimensionless) LL: Pipe length (m) QQ: Volumetric flow rate (m3/sm^3/s) DD: Internal diameter (m) gg: Acceleration due gravity (m/s2m/s^2)

title: Darcy-Weisbach Equations
text: friction factor depends Reynolds number relative roughness pipe. hf package provides two distinct functions calculate : calc_friction_cw(): Uses implicit Colebrook-White equation (default). finds exact root iteratively. calc_friction_sj(): Uses explicit Swamee-Jain equation, highly accurate approximation runs faster massive datasets.
code: # Flow parameters Re <- 100000        # Reynolds number e <- 0.00026        # Absolute roughness (m) D <- 0.1            # Diameter (m)  # Compare both methods calc_friction_cw(reynolds = Re, roughness = e, diameter = D) #> [1] 0.02657412 calc_friction_sj(reynolds = Re, roughness = e, diameter = D) #> [1] 0.02681396

what: 2. Calculating Head Loss with Functional Injection
title: Darcy-Weisbach Equations
text: calc_head_loss_darcy function calculates head loss, can inject friction function want use via friction_fun argument.
code: # 1. Default approach (Uses Colebrook-White automatically) calc_head_loss_darcy(   length = 100, flow = 0.02, diameter = 0.1, roughness = 0.00026 ) #> [1] 8.513127  # 2. Functional Injection (Using Swamee-Jain) calc_head_loss_darcy(   length = 100, flow = 0.02, diameter = 0.1, roughness = 0.00026,   friction_fun = calc_friction_sj ) #> [1] 8.55815  # 3. Direct Value (If you already know the friction factor) calc_head_loss_darcy(   length = 100, flow = 0.02, diameter = 0.1, friction_factor = 0.025 ) #> [1] 8.262686

title: Darcy-Weisbach Equations
text: friction factor creates circular dependency diameter flow rate, hf package uses internal numerical solvers (uniroot) find exact values. Functional injection works well.
code: # Calculate the required diameter for a target head loss of 8.56m calc_diameter_darcy(   loss = 8.56, length = 100, flow = 0.02, roughness = 0.00026 ) #> [1] 0.09989527  # Calculate the maximum flow rate for the same head loss calc_flow_darcy(   loss = 8.56, length = 100, diameter = 0.1, roughness = 0.00026 ) #> [1] 0.02005564

title: Equações de Darcy-Weisbach
text: equação de Darcy-Weisbach é fórmula universal e teoricamente mais rigorosa para o cálculo da perda de carga em condutos forçados. Ao contrário de fórmulas empíricas como de Hazen-Williams, equação de Darcy-Weisbach é aplicável qualquer fluido e qualquer regime de escoamento (laminar, transição ou turbulento). pacote hf, equação baseia-se na vazão volumétrica (QQ): hf=f8LQ2π2gD5h_f = f \frac{8 L Q^2}{\pi^2 g D^5} Em que: hfh_f: Perda de carga distribuída (m) ff: Fator de atrito de Darcy (adimensional) LL: Comprimento da tubulação (m) QQ: Vazão volumétrica (m3/sm^3/s) DD: Diâmetro interno (m) gg: Aceleração da gravidade (m/s2m/s^2)

title: Equações de Darcy-Weisbach
text: O fator de atrito depende Número de Reynolds e da rugosidade relativa tubo. O pacote hf fornece duas funções distintas para calculá-lo: calc_friction_cw(): Utiliza equação implícita de Colebrook-White (padrão). Encontra raiz exata iterativamente. calc_friction_sj(): Utiliza equação explícita de Swamee-Jain, uma aproximação de alta precisão que não requer iteração.
code: # Parâmetros do escoamento Re <- 100000        # Número de Reynolds e <- 0.00026        # Rugosidade absoluta (m) D <- 0.1            # Diâmetro (m)  # Comparando ambos os métodos calc_friction_cw(reynolds = Re, roughness = e, diameter = D) #> [1] 0.02657412 calc_friction_sj(reynolds = Re, roughness = e, diameter = D) #> [1] 0.02681396

what: 2. Calculando a Perda de Carga com Injeção Funcional
title: Equações de Darcy-Weisbach
text: função calc_head_loss_darcy calcula perda, mas você pode “injetar” função de atrito que deseja utilizar através argumento friction_fun.
code: # 1. Padrão (Usa Colebrook-White automaticamente) calc_head_loss_darcy(   length = 100, flow = 0.02, diameter = 0.1, roughness = 0.00026 ) #> [1] 8.513127  # 2. Injeção Funcional (Usando Swamee-Jain) calc_head_loss_darcy(   length = 100, flow = 0.02, diameter = 0.1, roughness = 0.00026,   friction_fun = calc_friction_sj ) #> [1] 8.55815  # 3. Valor Direto (Se você já calculou o fator de atrito) calc_head_loss_darcy(   length = 100, flow = 0.02, diameter = 0.1, friction_factor = 0.025 ) #> [1] 8.262686

title: Equações de Darcy-Weisbach
text: Como o fator de atrito cria uma dependência circular matemática, o pacote hf utiliza solucionadores numéricos internos (uniroot) para encontrar os valores exatos de diâmetro e vazão. injeção funcional também funciona nestas funções.
code: # Calcular o diâmetro necessário para uma perda de 8.56m calc_diameter_darcy(   loss = 8.56, length = 100, flow = 0.02, roughness = 0.00026 ) #> [1] 0.09989527  # Calcular a vazão máxima para a mesma perda de carga calc_flow_darcy(   loss = 8.56, length = 100, diameter = 0.1, roughness = 0.00026 ) #> [1] 0.02005564

title: Flamant Head Loss
text: Flamant equation empirical formula widely used agricultural hydraulics, particularly micro-irrigation systems (drip micro-sprinkler irrigation). highly accurate calculating friction head loss small-diameter pipes (typically 50 mm) made smooth materials like PVC Polyethylene (PE).

title: Flamant Head Loss
text: bb factor represents internal roughness pipe according Flamant’s experiments. smooth plastic pipes (PVC, PE), standard value 0.0001350.000135.

title: Flamant Head Loss
text: base mathematical equation head loss (hfh_f) metric system : hf=6.107⋅b⋅L⋅Q1.75D4.75h_f = 6.107 \cdot b \cdot \frac{L \cdot Q^{1.75}}{D^{4.75}} : hfh_f: Friction head loss (m) LL: Pipe length (m) QQ: Volumetric flow rate (m3/sm^3/s) bb: Flamant roughness coefficient (dimensionless) DD: Internal diameter (m)

title: Flamant Head Loss
text: calculate friction head loss, use calc_head_loss_flamant() function. Let’s calculate expected head loss 50-meter PE pipe (b=0.000135b = 0.000135) internal diameter 0.015 meters (15 mm) carrying flow 0.0002m3/s0.0002\ m^3/s (0.2 L/s).

title: Flamant Head Loss
text: know maximum allowable head loss lateral irrigation line, can determine required minimum pipe diameter using calc_diameter_flamant(). Suppose system allows maximum head loss 1.5 meters 50m length 0.0002m3/s0.0002\ m^3/s flow rate.

title: Flamant Head Loss
text: find maximum flow rate small pipe can deliver given available pressure head, use calc_flow_flamant() function.

title: Perda de Carga de Flamant
text: equação de Flamant é uma fórmula empírica amplamente utilizada na hidráulica agrícola, especialmente em projetos de irrigação localizada (gotejamento e microaspersão). Ela é altamente precisa para o cálculo da perda de carga em tubulações de pequeno diâmetro (tipicamente inferiores 50 mm) feitas de materiais lisos como PVC e Polietileno (PE).

title: Perda de Carga de Flamant
text: O fator bb representa rugosidade interna da tubulação de acordo com os experimentos de Flamant. Para tubos plásticos lisos (PVC, PE), o valor padrão é 0.0001350.000135.

title: Perda de Carga de Flamant
text: equação matemática base para perda de carga (hfh_f) Sistema Internacional é: hf=6.107⋅b⋅L⋅Q1.75D4.75h_f = 6.107 \cdot b \cdot \frac{L \cdot Q^{1.75}}{D^{4.75}} Em que: hfh_f: Perda de carga (m) LL: Comprimento da tubulação (m) QQ: Vazão volumétrica (m3/sm^3/s) bb: Coeficiente de rugosidade de Flamant (adimensional) DD: Diâmetro interno (m)

title: Perda de Carga de Flamant
text: Para calcular perda de carga, utilize função calc_head_loss_flamant(). Vamos calcular perda esperada para um tubo de PE (b=0.000135b = 0.000135) de 50 metros de comprimento, com diâmetro interno de 0,015 metros (15 mm), transportando uma vazão de 0.0002m3/s0.0002\ m^3/s (0,2 L/s).

title: Perda de Carga de Flamant
text: Se o projeto estipula uma perda de carga máxima permitida para linha lateral de irrigação, você pode determinar o diâmetro mínimo necessário utilizando calc_diameter_flamant(). Suponha que o sistema permite uma perda de carga máxima de 1,5 metros para 50m de comprimento e vazão de 0.0002m3/s0.0002\ m^3/s.

title: Perda de Carga de Flamant
text: Para descobrir vazão máxima que um tubo de pequeno diâmetro pode entregar dada uma pressão disponível, use função calc_flow_flamant().

title: Friction Factors in the hf Package
text: Darcy friction factor (ff) key parameter calculating head loss. hf package uses functional programming approach, allowing users “inject” different calculation methods Darcy-Weisbach functions.

title: Friction Factors in the hf Package
text: industry standard turbulent flow rough pipes. implicit equation, hf solves using numerical root-finding (uniroot). 1f=−2log10(ϵ3.7D+2.51Ref) \frac{1}{\sqrt{f}} = -2 \log_{10} \left( \frac{\epsilon}{3.7D} + \frac{2.51}{Re\sqrt{f}} \right)

title: Friction Factors in the hf Package
text: highly accurate explicit approximation Colebrook-White equation. Recommended high-performance calculations numerical iteration slow. f=0.25[log10(ϵ3.7D+5.74Re0.9)]2f = \frac{0.25}{\left[ \log_{10} \left( \frac{\epsilon}{3.7D} + \frac{5.74}{Re^{0.9}} \right) \right]^2}

title: Friction Factors in the hf Package
text: empirical formula smooth pipes Reynolds numbers 10510^5. provides simpler alternative relative roughness negligible. f=0.3164Re0.25 f = \frac{0.3164}{Re^{0.25}}

title: Fatores de Atrito no Pacote hf
text: O fator de atrito de Darcy (ff) é um parâmetro adimensional essencial para o cálculo da perda de carga pela equação de Darcy-Weisbach. pacote hf, adotamos uma abordagem de injeção funcional, permitindo que o usuário escolha o método de cálculo de ff mais adequado ao seu problema.

title: Fatores de Atrito no Pacote hf
text: Considerada equação de referência para escoamento turbulento em tubos rugosos. Por ser implícita, o pacote utiliza o método de uniroot para encontrar solução exata. 1f=−2log10(ϵ3.7D+2.51Ref) \frac{1}{\sqrt{f}} = -2 \log_{10} \left( \frac{\epsilon}{3.7D} + \frac{2.51}{Re\sqrt{f}} \right)

title: Fatores de Atrito no Pacote hf
text: Uma das aproximações explícitas mais precisas para equação de Colebrook-White. É ideal para processamento de grandes volumes de dados por não exigir iteração numérica. f=0.25[log10(ϵ3.7D+5.74Re0.9)]2f = \frac{0.25}{\left[ \log_{10} \left( \frac{\epsilon}{3.7D} + \frac{5.74}{Re^{0.9}} \right) \right]^2}

title: Fatores de Atrito no Pacote hf
text: Indicada para tubos perfeitamente lisos (como PVC novo ou vidro) e números de Reynolds até 10510^5. f=0.3164Re0.25 f = \frac{0.3164}{Re^{0.25}}

title: Hazen-Williams Head Loss
text: Hazen-Williams equation empirical formula widely used hydraulics calculate friction head loss pipes. Developed early 20th century Allen Hazen Gardner Williams, primary advantage Darcy-Weisbach equation simplicity: roughness coefficient (CC) relatively constant given pipe material, regardless flow conditions (Reynolds number).

title: Hazen-Williams Head Loss
text: critical note Hazen-Williams equation valid water ordinary temperatures (typically 5∘C5^\circ C 25∘C25^\circ C) flowing standard velocities. used fluids, hot water, extreme flow regimes.

title: Hazen-Williams Head Loss
text: CC factor represents internal smoothness pipe. Higher values indicate smoother pipes less friction: PVC Smooth Plastic: C≈140C \approx 140 150150 New Cast Iron Steel: C≈130C \approx 130 Old, Tuberculated Cast Iron: C≈100C \approx 100

title: Hazen-Williams Head Loss
text: base mathematical equation head loss (hfh_f) metric system : hf=10.67⋅L⋅(Q/C)1.852D4.87h_f = \frac{10.67 \cdot L \cdot (Q/C)^{1.852}}{D^{4.87}} : hfh_f: Friction head loss (m) LL: Pipe length (m) QQ: Volumetric flow rate (m3/sm^3/s) CC: Hazen-Williams roughness coefficient (dimensionless) DD: Internal diameter (m)

title: Hazen-Williams Head Loss
text: calculate friction head loss, use calc_head_loss_hw() function. Let’s calculate expected head loss 150-meter PVC pipe (C=150C = 150) internal diameter 0.1 meters (100 mm) carrying flow 0.025 m3/sm^3/s (25 L/s).

title: Hazen-Williams Head Loss
text: know maximum allowable head loss hydraulic system, can determine required minimum pipe diameter using calc_diameter_hw(). Suppose system allows maximum head loss 5 meters 150m length 0.025 m3/sm^3/s flow rate.

title: Hazen-Williams Head Loss
text: find maximum flow rate pipe can deliver given available pressure head, use calc_flow_hw() function. Let’s find much water flows 100m pipe 0.15m diameter, roughness coefficient 140, available head 3 meters.

title: Perda de Carga de Hazen-Williams
text: equação de Hazen-Williams é uma fórmula empírica amplamente utilizada na hidráulica para calcular perda de carga distribuída em tubulações. Desenvolvida início século XX por Allen Hazen e Gardner Williams, sua principal vantagem sobre equação de Darcy-Weisbach é simplicidade: o coeficiente de rugosidade (CC) é relativamente constante para um determinado material, independentemente das condições de escoamento (Número de Reynolds).

title: Perda de Carga de Hazen-Williams
text: É fundamental notar que equação de Hazen-Williams é válida apenas para água em temperaturas ambientes (tipicamente entre 5∘C5^\circ C e 25∘C25^\circ C) escoando velocidades normais. Ela não deve ser utilizada para outros fluidos, água muito quente ou regimes de escoamento extremos.

title: Perda de Carga de Hazen-Williams
text: O fator CC representa lisura interna da tubulação. Valores maiores indicam tubos mais lisos e com menos atrito: PVC ou Plástico liso: C≈140C \approx 140 150150 Ferro Fundido Novo ou Aço: C≈130C \approx 130 Ferro Fundido Antigo (com incrustações): C≈100C \approx 100

title: Perda de Carga de Hazen-Williams
text: equação matemática base para perda de carga (hfh_f) Sistema Internacional é: hf=10.67⋅L⋅(Q/C)1.852D4.87h_f = \frac{10.67 \cdot L \cdot (Q/C)^{1.852}}{D^{4.87}} Em que: hfh_f: Perda de carga (m) LL: Comprimento da tubulação (m) QQ: Vazão volumétrica (m3/sm^3/s) CC: Coeficiente de rugosidade de Hazen-Williams (adimensional) DD: Diâmetro interno (m)

title: Perda de Carga de Hazen-Williams
text: Para calcular perda de carga, utilize função calc_head_loss_hw(). Vamos calcular perda esperada para um tubo de PVC (C=150C = 150) de 150 metros de comprimento, com diâmetro interno de 0,1 metros (100 mm), transportando uma vazão de 0,025 m3/sm^3/s (25 L/s).

title: Perda de Carga de Hazen-Williams
text: Se o projeto estipula uma perda de carga máxima permitida, você pode determinar o diâmetro mínimo necessário utilizando calc_diameter_hw(). Suponha que o sistema permite uma perda de carga máxima de 5 metros para os mesmos 150m de comprimento e vazão de 0,025 m3/sm^3/s.

title: Perda de Carga de Hazen-Williams
text: Para descobrir vazão máxima que um tubo pode entregar dada uma pressão disponível, use função calc_flow_hw(). Vamos verificar quanta água flui por um tubo de 100m com 0,15m de diâmetro, coeficiente de rugosidade de 140 e uma carga disponível (perda) de 3 metros.

text: Tolentino Jr. J (2026). hf: Head Loss Calculations Pipelines. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://github.com/joaobtj/hf.
code: @Manual{,   title = {hf: Head Loss Calculations for Pipelines},   author = {Joao Batista {Tolentino Jr.}},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/joaobtj/hf}, }

title: Head Loss Calculations for Pipelines
text: hf package provides simple, efficient, mathematically rigorous functions calculate friction head loss, flow rate, required pipe diameters using standard hydraulic equations. fully supports universal Darcy-Weisbach equation (Colebrook-White, Swamee-Jain, friction factors) empirical equations (Hazen-Williams Flamant), making ideal tool agronomic engineering hydraulic designs.

title: Head Loss Calculations for Pipelines
text: can try package directly browser without installing R! hf package includes serverless Shiny application powered WebAssembly (shinylive), allowing perform complex hydraulic calculations fly. interactive app, can: Solve variable: Instantly calculate Head Loss, Flow Rate, Required Pipe Diameter based system parameters. Compare Methods: Switch seamlessly universal Darcy-Weisbach equation (testing Colebrook-White Swamee-Jain factors) empirical Hazen-Williams formula. Visualize System Dynamics: View automatically generated plots, including System Curves (Head Loss vs. Flow Rate) Friction Sensitivity (Head Loss vs. Pipe Diameter) understand pipeline behaves. Zero Installation: app runs entirely client-side browser R backend required. 👉 Try hf Hydraulic Calculator

title: Head Loss Calculations for Pipelines
text: can install development version hf GitHub : Alternatively, can install via R-Universe:

title: Head Loss Calculations for Pipelines
text: universal Darcy-Weisbach method allows calculation head loss using different friction factor functions. default, uses implicit Colebrook-White equation.
code: library(hf)  # Calculate head loss (m) for a 100m pipe, 0.02 m³/s flow, 0.1m diameter calc_head_loss_darcy(   length = 100,   flow = 0.02,   diameter = 0.1,   roughness = 0.00026 ) #> [1] 8.513127  # Use functional injection to apply the Swamee-Jain approximation calc_head_loss_darcy(   length = 100,   flow = 0.02,   diameter = 0.1,   roughness = 0.00026,   friction_fun = calc_friction_sj ) #> [1] 8.55815

title: Head Loss Calculations for Pipelines
text: full documentation bilingual tutorials (English Portuguese), visit package website: joaobtj.github.io/hf

title: Head Loss Calculations for Pipelines

what: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_diameter_darcy
title: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_diameter_darcy
text: Calculates required internal pipe diameter iteratively given specific head loss flow rate. taking functional approach, supports friction factor calculation function.

title: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_diameter_darcy
code: calc_diameter_darcy(   loss,   length,   flow,   roughness,   friction_fun = NULL,   viscosity = 1.004e-06,   gravity = 9.81 )

title: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_diameter_darcy
text: loss Numeric. target friction head loss (meters). length Numeric. length pipe (meters). flow Numeric. volumetric flow rate (cubic meters per second). roughness Numeric. absolute internal roughness pipe (meters). Required unless friction_factor provided. friction_fun Function. function calculate friction factor (must accept reynolds, roughness, diameter). NULL (default), uses calc_friction_cw. viscosity Numeric. Kinematic viscosity fluid (sq. meters per sec). Default 1.004e-6. gravity Numeric. Acceleration due gravity (meters per second squared). Default 9.81.

title: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_diameter_darcy
text: numeric vector representing required internal diameter meters.

title: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_diameter_darcy
code: # Find diameter using the default Colebrook-White function calc_diameter_darcy(loss = 5, length = 100, flow = 0.02, roughness = 0.00026) #> [1] 0.1107086  # Find diameter by injecting Swamee-Jain calc_diameter_darcy(   loss = 5, length = 100, flow = 0.02, roughness = 0.00026,   friction_fun = calc_friction_sj ) #> [1] 0.1108447

what: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Flamant — calc_diameter_flamant
title: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Flamant — calc_diameter_flamant
text: Calculates required internal pipe diameter given specific target head loss, length, flow rate, Flamant roughness coefficient.

title: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Flamant — calc_diameter_flamant
Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Flamant — calc_diameter_flamant
text: loss Numeric. target friction head loss (meters). length Numeric. length pipe (meters). flow Numeric. volumetric flow rate (cubic meters per second). coef Numeric. Flamant roughness coefficient (b). Default 0.000135, typical smooth plastic pipes (e.g., PVC, PE).

title: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Flamant — calc_diameter_flamant
text: numeric vector representing required internal diameter meters.

title: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Flamant — calc_diameter_flamant
code: # Find diameter for a 50m pipe with 1.5m allowable head loss and 0.0002 m^3/s flow calc_diameter_flamant(loss = 1.5, length = 50, flow = 0.0002) #> [1] 0.0203516

what: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Hazen-Williams — calc_diameter_hw
title: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Hazen-Williams — calc_diameter_hw
text: Calculates required internal pipe diameter given specific target head loss, length, flow rate, roughness coefficient based Hazen-Williams (hw) equation.

title: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Hazen-Williams — calc_diameter_hw
Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Hazen-Williams — calc_diameter_hw
text: loss Numeric. target friction head loss (meters). length Numeric. length pipe (meters). flow Numeric. volumetric flow rate (cubic meters per second). coef Numeric. Hazen-Williams roughness coefficient (dimensionless). Default 140, typical PVC pipes.

title: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Hazen-Williams — calc_diameter_hw
text: numeric vector representing required internal diameter meters.

title: Calculate Required Pipe Diameter using Hazen-Williams — calc_diameter_hw
code: # Find diameter for a 100m pipe with 2m allowable head loss, 0.02 m^3/s flow (C = 140) calc_diameter_hw(loss = 2, length = 100, flow = 0.02) #> [1] 0.1252402

what: Calculate Flow Rate using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_flow_darcy
title: Calculate Flow Rate using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_flow_darcy
text: Calculates volumetric flow rate iteratively given specific head loss. function uses functional programming approach, allowing injection friction factor function (e.g., Colebrook-White).

title: Calculate Flow Rate using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_flow_darcy
code: calc_flow_darcy(   loss,   length,   diameter,   roughness,   friction_fun = NULL,   viscosity = 1.004e-06,   gravity = 9.81 )

title: Calculate Flow Rate using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_flow_darcy
text: loss Numeric. target friction head loss (meters). length Numeric. length pipe (meters). diameter Numeric. internal diameter pipe (meters). roughness Numeric. absolute internal roughness pipe (meters). Required unless friction_factor provided. friction_fun Function. function calculate friction factor (must accept reynolds, roughness, diameter). NULL (default), uses calc_friction_cw. viscosity Numeric. Kinematic viscosity fluid (sq. meters per sec). Default 1.004e-6. gravity Numeric. Acceleration due gravity (meters per second squared). Default 9.81.

title: Calculate Flow Rate using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_flow_darcy
text: numeric vector representing volumetric flow rate cubic meters per second.

title: Calculate Flow Rate using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_flow_darcy
code: # Default: Uses Colebrook-White to find the flow rate calc_flow_darcy(loss = 5, length = 100, diameter = 0.1, roughness = 0.00026) #> [1] 0.01527351  # Injecting Swamee-Jain function calc_flow_darcy(   loss = 5, length = 100, diameter = 0.1, roughness = 0.00026,   friction_fun = calc_friction_sj ) #> [1] 0.01522819

what: Calculate Flow Rate using Flamant — calc_flow_flamant
title: Calculate Flow Rate using Flamant — calc_flow_flamant
text: Calculates volumetric flow rate pipe given specific head loss, length, internal diameter, Flamant coefficient.

title: Calculate Flow Rate using Flamant — calc_flow_flamant
Calculate Flow Rate using Flamant — calc_flow_flamant
text: loss Numeric. friction head loss (meters). length Numeric. length pipe (meters). diameter Numeric. internal diameter pipe (meters). coef Numeric. Flamant roughness coefficient (b). Default 0.000135, typical smooth plastic pipes (e.g., PVC, PE).

title: Calculate Flow Rate using Flamant — calc_flow_flamant
text: numeric vector representing volumetric flow rate cubic meters per second.

title: Calculate Flow Rate using Flamant — calc_flow_flamant
code: # Find maximum flow rate for a 50m pipe with 15mm diameter and 1.5m head loss calc_flow_flamant(loss = 1.5, length = 50, diameter = 0.015) #> [1] 8.737103e-05

what: Calculate Flow Rate using Hazen-Williams — calc_flow_hw
title: Calculate Flow Rate using Hazen-Williams — calc_flow_hw
text: Calculates volumetric flow rate pipe given specific head loss, length, internal diameter, roughness coefficient based Hazen-Williams (hw) equation.

title: Calculate Flow Rate using Hazen-Williams — calc_flow_hw
Calculate Flow Rate using Hazen-Williams — calc_flow_hw
text: loss Numeric. friction head loss (meters). length Numeric. length pipe (meters). diameter Numeric. internal diameter pipe (meters). coef Numeric. Hazen-Williams roughness coefficient (dimensionless). Default 140, typical PVC pipes.

title: Calculate Flow Rate using Hazen-Williams — calc_flow_hw
text: numeric vector representing volumetric flow rate cubic meters per second.

title: Calculate Flow Rate using Hazen-Williams — calc_flow_hw
code: # Find flow rate for a 100m pipe with 0.1m diameter, C = 140, and 2m head loss calc_flow_hw(loss = 2, length = 100, diameter = 0.1) #> [1] 0.01106633

what: Calculate Friction Factor using Blasius — calc_friction_blasius
title: Calculate Friction Factor using Blasius — calc_friction_blasius
text: Calculates Darcy friction factor using empirical Blasius equation. formula highly accurate smooth pipes (e.g., PVC, glass) turbulent flows Reynolds numbers 100,000.

title: Calculate Friction Factor using Blasius — calc_friction_blasius
Calculate Friction Factor using Blasius — calc_friction_blasius
text: reynolds Numeric. Reynolds number flow (dimensionless). roughness Numeric. absolute internal roughness pipe (meters). diameter Numeric. internal diameter pipe (meters).

title: Calculate Friction Factor using Blasius — calc_friction_blasius
text: numeric vector representing Darcy friction factor.

title: Calculate Friction Factor using Blasius — calc_friction_blasius
code: # Calculate friction factor for a Reynolds number of 50,000 calc_friction_blasius(reynolds = 50000, roughness = 0, diameter = 0.1) #> [1] 0.02115894

what: Calculate Friction Factor using Colebrook-White — calc_friction_cw
title: Calculate Friction Factor using Colebrook-White — calc_friction_cw
text: Calculates Darcy friction factor iteratively using implicit Colebrook-White equation turbulent flow. laminar flow (Re <= 2000), returns exact solution (64 / Re).

title: Calculate Friction Factor using Colebrook-White — calc_friction_cw
Calculate Friction Factor using Colebrook-White — calc_friction_cw
text: reynolds Numeric. Reynolds number flow (dimensionless). roughness Numeric. absolute internal roughness pipe (meters). diameter Numeric. internal diameter pipe (meters).

title: Calculate Friction Factor using Colebrook-White — calc_friction_cw
text: numeric vector representing Darcy friction factor.

title: Calculate Friction Factor using Colebrook-White — calc_friction_cw

what: Calculate Friction Factor using Swamee-Jain — calc_friction_sj
title: Calculate Friction Factor using Swamee-Jain — calc_friction_sj
text: Calculates Darcy friction factor using explicit Swamee-Jain equation. highly accurate approximation Colebrook-White equation require iteration.

title: Calculate Friction Factor using Swamee-Jain — calc_friction_sj
Calculate Friction Factor using Swamee-Jain — calc_friction_sj
text: reynolds Numeric. Reynolds number flow (dimensionless). roughness Numeric. absolute internal roughness pipe (meters). diameter Numeric. internal diameter pipe (meters).

title: Calculate Friction Factor using Swamee-Jain — calc_friction_sj
text: numeric vector representing Darcy friction factor.

title: Calculate Friction Factor using Swamee-Jain — calc_friction_sj

what: Calculate Head Loss using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_head_loss_darcy
title: Calculate Head Loss using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_head_loss_darcy
text: Calculates friction head loss pipe based universal Darcy-Weisbach equation. function allowing user inject friction factor calculation function (e.g., Colebrook-White Swamee-Jain). Alternatively, pre-calculated friction factor can provided directly.

title: Calculate Head Loss using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_head_loss_darcy
code: calc_head_loss_darcy(   length,   flow,   diameter,   roughness = NULL,   friction_factor = NULL,   friction_fun = NULL,   viscosity = 1.004e-06,   gravity = 9.81 )

title: Calculate Head Loss using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_head_loss_darcy
text: length Numeric. length pipe (meters). flow Numeric. volumetric flow rate (cubic meters per second). diameter Numeric. internal diameter pipe (meters). roughness Numeric. absolute internal roughness pipe (meters). Required unless friction_factor provided. friction_factor Numeric. optional pre-calculated Darcy friction factor. provided, roughness friction_fun ignored. friction_fun Function. function calculate friction factor (must accept reynolds, roughness, diameter). NULL (default), uses calc_friction_cw. viscosity Numeric. Kinematic viscosity fluid (sq. meters per sec). Default 1.004e-6. gravity Numeric. Acceleration due gravity (meters per second squared). Default 9.81.

title: Calculate Head Loss using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_head_loss_darcy
text: numeric vector representing head loss meters.

title: Calculate Head Loss using Darcy-Weisbach — calc_head_loss_darcy
code: # 1. Default: Uses Colebrook-White function automatically calc_head_loss_darcy(length = 100, flow = 0.02, diameter = 0.1, roughness = 0.00026) #> [1] 8.513127  # 2. Functional Injection: Pass the Swamee-Jain function as an argument calc_head_loss_darcy(   length = 100,   flow = 0.02,   diameter = 0.1,   roughness = 0.00026,   friction_fun = calc_friction_sj ) #> [1] 8.55815  # 3. Direct Value: Provide the friction factor manually calc_head_loss_darcy(length = 100, flow = 0.02, diameter = 0.1, friction_factor = 0.02) #> [1] 6.610149

what: Calculate Head Loss using Flamant Equation — calc_head_loss_flamant
title: Calculate Head Loss using Flamant Equation — calc_head_loss_flamant
text: Calculates friction head loss pipe based empirical Flamant equation. formula highly recommended small-diameter plastic pipes (typically < 50 mm) commonly used micro-irrigation systems.

title: Calculate Head Loss using Flamant Equation — calc_head_loss_flamant
Calculate Head Loss using Flamant Equation — calc_head_loss_flamant
text: length Numeric. length pipe (meters). flow Numeric. volumetric flow rate (cubic meters per second). diameter Numeric. internal diameter pipe (meters). coef Numeric. Flamant roughness coefficient (b). Default 0.000135, typical smooth plastic pipes (e.g., PVC, PE).

title: Calculate Head Loss using Flamant Equation — calc_head_loss_flamant
text: numeric vector representing head loss meters.

title: Calculate Head Loss using Flamant Equation — calc_head_loss_flamant
code: # Calculate head loss for a 50m PE pipe with 15mm diameter and 0.2 L/s flow calc_head_loss_flamant(length = 50, flow = 0.0002, diameter = 0.015) #> [1] 6.389998

what: Calculate Head Loss using Hazen-Williams Equation — calc_head_loss_hw
title: Calculate Head Loss using Hazen-Williams Equation — calc_head_loss_hw
text: Calculates friction head loss pipe based empirical Hazen-Williams (hw) equation. valid water ordinary temperatures.

title: Calculate Head Loss using Hazen-Williams Equation — calc_head_loss_hw
Calculate Head Loss using Hazen-Williams Equation — calc_head_loss_hw
text: length Numeric. length pipe (meters). flow Numeric. volumetric flow rate (cubic meters per second). diameter Numeric. internal diameter pipe (meters). coef Numeric. Hazen-Williams roughness coefficient (dimensionless). Default 140, typical PVC pipes.

title: Calculate Head Loss using Hazen-Williams Equation — calc_head_loss_hw
text: numeric vector representing head loss meters.

title: Calculate Head Loss using Hazen-Williams Equation — calc_head_loss_hw
code: # Calculate head loss for a 100m PVC pipe (C = 140) with 0.1m diameter and 0.02 m^3/s flow calc_head_loss_hw(length = 100, flow = 0.02, diameter = 0.1) #> [1] 5.984706
